evidence.text: Seizure type and frequency: focal seizures with altered awareness every 3 weeks
Current anti-epileptic medication: lamotrigine 75mg bd (to reduce and stop as detailed below)
Currently she is taking sodium valproate 500 mg twice a day and levetiracetam 1000 mg twice today.
generalised tonic clonic seizures with myoclonic jerks
history is suggestive of juvenile myoclonic epilepsy
the smaller episodes are focal seizures with altered awareness
the episodes during sleep are focal to bilateral convulsive seizures
She gets generalised seizures once a week
The absences and jerks happen more frequently
Sodium Valproate 200 mg twice a day
She also gets tonic clonic seizures
generalised tonic clonic seizures, 1 since previous appointment
Current anti epileptic medication: 	lamotrigine 100 mg BD
I think that the jerks are myoclonic seizures
Symptomatic structural epilepsy secondary probably caused by early life meningitis
she is having more generalised tonic clinic seizures
Increase the carbamazepine to 600mg twice a day.
Seizure type and frequency: 	Focal onset convulsive seizure
Current anti-epileptic medication:	Lamotrigine 100mg bd (to increase as detailed below)
She does continue to take sodium valproate 200 milligrammes twice a day.
New diagnosis of epilepsy with generalised tonic clonic seizures from sleep

values.normalized_value: epilepsy with generalized tonic clonic seizures on awakening

evidence.text: Levetiracetam 250mgs once a day, increasing by 250mgs every 2 weeks to 500mgs bd
Medication: sodium valproate 500 milligrams mane 700 milligrams nocte
Diagnosis: probably JME presenting with generalised tonic seizures, absences and myoclonic jerks
